findings.title: CVE-2026-4670 / CVE-2026-5174 — Progress MOVEit Automation Pre-Auth Authentication Bypass and Privilege Escalation
findings.summary: Airbus Seclab disclosed pre-auth authentication bypass (CVE-2026-4670, CVSS 9.8) plus auth-to-admin privilege escalation (CVE-2026-5174, 7.7) in Progress MOVEit Automation, patched 2026-04-30 — Cl0p's 2023 MOVEit history makes rapid weaponisation likely.

findings.title: Google Gemini CLI Headless-Mode Pre-Sandbox RCE (CVSS 10.0)
findings.summary: Gemini CLI in headless mode implicitly trusted workspace .gemini/settings.json and executed attacker-controlled MCP/shell commands pre-sandbox; a malicious PR plants the file and runs code on the GitHub Actions runner with workflow secrets — patched 2026-04-30 by requiring explicit workspace trust.

findings.title: SonicWall SonicOS April 2026 Roll-Up — Eight CVEs Including CVE-2026-0204 Management-Function Access-Control Bypass
findings.affected_technology: SonicWall NSA / TZ / NSv firewall appliances (SonicOS, 33 product variants)
findings.summary: Coordinated 2026-04-30 SonicOS advisory: management access-control bypass (CVE-2026-0204, 8.0), path traversal to restricted management services (CVE-2026-0205, 6.8), and six post-auth DoS / memory-corruption crashes — apply combined firmware update.

findings.title: CVE-2026-41940 cPanel & WHM Authentication Bypass — Public PoC and IOC Scanner Now Circulating
findings.affected_technology: cPanel & WHM (all currently supported branches) + WP Squared 11.136.1
findings.summary: ≥6 weaponised PoC repos and an IOC scanner for CVE-2026-41940 appeared on GitHub within 24h of disclosure, dropping the bar from researcher-grade to commodity-botnet exploitation — expect mass scanning of TCP/2083 + 2087 imminently.

findings.title: Mini Shai-Hulud — Campaign Expands from npm/SAP CAP to PyPI via PyTorch Lightning 2.6.2 / 2.6.3
findings.affected_technology: PyPI package pytorch-lightning 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 (Python ecosystem extension of yesterday's npm SAP CAP attack)
findings.summary: TeamPCP Mini Shai-Hulud extended to PyPI 2026-04-30 with trojanised pytorch-lightning 2.6.2/2.6.3 that runs the Bun stealer on import (not preinstall) and uses commits impersonating Claude Code for blend-in, widening blast radius to ML pipelines.

findings.title: CVE-2026-32202 Windows Shell — Added to CISA KEV Catalog 2026-04-29
findings.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-32202 to KEV on 2026-04-29 (BOD 22-01 21-day federal patching mandate), codifying yesterday's APT28 active-exploitation confirmation as a compliance trigger.

noted.summary: Out-of-remote-services scope but ≥6 distinct PoC repos updated within the past hour — schedule a kernel-update window for jump hosts, container hosts, and shared CI runners (patched in 5.10.254+ / 5.15.204+ / 6.1.170+ / 6.6.137+).
Newly added to CISA KEV 2026-04-29 (CVSS 8.4 path traversal); old 2024 bug — confirm the 2024 patch is applied if you still operate ScreenConnect on-prem.
DoS / memory-corruption in SBC codec, RDP dissector, and profile-import path (CVSS 7.0–7.8) — analyst-tool scope, not internet-facing infra; routine update on SOC analyst workstations.
RCE via malicious slash commands (CVSS 8.8) — yet another AI-agent CLI in the unauth-RCE-via-AI-tooling pattern alongside nginx-ui, mcp-atlassian, chatgpt-mcp-server.
Authorization bypass (CVSS 6.3) — many security teams self-host DefectDojo for AppSec triage, schedule the patch on internal AppSec tooling.
Authorization flaw (CVSS 7.3) in a niche workflow plugin — naming overlap with OpenClaw is incidental (different vendor), mention to avoid confusion.
Vulnerability (CVSS 7.2) in the widely used Python CLI library — review what your in-house tooling exposes.
Developer-IDE issue (CVSS 7.4); client-only, but worth noting in the AI-IDE / developer-tool security thread alongside Gemini CLI and Mini Shai-Hulud.
Buffer overflow in wireless config function (CVSS 8.8); niche IoT/router population.
SQL injection (CVSS 7.3); very niche, mention for completeness.
Routing bypass (CVSS 5.0); lower severity, worth tracking on enterprise PaaS deployments.
Inc Ransom group, 170,000 records, disclosed nearly a year after the breach — useful incident-response reference.
Data exfiltrated 2026-03-30, malicious code published one week later; if confirmed as a distinct supply-chain vector against AppSec vendor tooling, will promote next cycle.
AI-driven vuln-discovery announcement (Schneier 2026-04-29); track for upcoming Mozilla advisory wave and AI-vulnscan trend.
40+ templates with AI campaign generator observed 2026-04-30; brief SOC anti-phishing detection team.

noted.product: TP-Link routers (via Brazilian 'Huge Networks' DDoS-for-hire abuse)
noted.summary: Krebs investigation 2026-04-30 — Python attack scripts targeting CVE-2023-1389; trend signal for that CVE plus an example of compromised security-vendor infrastructure.
